mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding corporate revenues and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9% indicates severe labor market slack that crushes consumer spending and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising payroll employment and a near-zero federal funds rate generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days because labor market recovery combined with anchored policy rates lifts equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and FEDFUNDS shows a t-statistic below 2 in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a near-zero interest rate environment, month-over-month increases in housing starts predict positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because housing leads the business cycle and cheap financing amplifies construction activity.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 in the low-rate regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in consumer price inflation reduce real discount rates and squeeze profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes produce a Rank IC above -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in payroll employment signal contracting aggregate demand and falling corporate revenues, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month declines produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 2% triggers a sharp repricing of recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 40% of instances following a greater than 2% INDPRO drop.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a near-zero federal funds rate generate positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days because deeply negative real rates push investors out of cash and into equities.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS shows a t-statistic below 2 in a rolling 3-year regression.
